## [12:14 - 15:03]

only contain the offer a very plain page with just here's the logo, here's what's in it, click here to buy. The other one is the offer plus some sales elements. Let me explain each of these. The offer only gives you a list of the elements of the offer itself like the product, the bonuses, the guarantee, the price, and the buy button. Okay, very simple. That's all that's on the page, doesn't have a lot of fancy graphics, anything else, it just summarizes the offer. In the offer plus sales elements camp, you give everything that's in the offer only section, plus you might do some brief positioning, some testimonials, the benefits of frequently asked questions, and how your life will be different as a result of doing this. And so it's a, it's sort of a long form sales letter with a lot of the, a lot of the long form taken out. So you still got some elements of a sales letter, just not all of them. Personally, I prefer the offer plus sales elements, I find it sells better.
